detailedExemplarData.title: The relationship between the surface area of a pendulum and its amplitude in simple harmonic motion

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables, graphs and labels are presented in a clear logical sequence
Processing steps (peak averaging, exponential fits, linear regression of Î» vs. A, area uncertainty propagation) are appropriate and largely accurate
Uncertainties for instruments and error bars on final fit demonstrate consideration of errors
weaknesses: Uncertainty propagation for Î» is incomplete and no confidence intervals are shown on fits
Inconsistent significant figures and truncated axes reduce precision
Sign convention inconsistencies and lack of residual analysis limit depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data processing is carried out appropriately and accurately, satisfying the 5â6 band. Communication is clear but not fully precise due to formatting and graphing issues, and consideration of uncertainties is present but incomplete (missing propagated Î» errors), placing the work solidly at 5.

strengths: Research question is clearly formulated within a specific and appropriate physics context of damping and viscous drag
Introduction links theory to realâworld and personal motivations, fulfilling topâband context requirements
Detailed stepâbyâstep methodology with equipment lists, uncertainties and diagrams provides largely reproducible procedure
Controlled variables are identified and their effects on damping are described with justification
weaknesses: Minor ambiguities remain (exact string diameter/material, playâdoh mass distribution procedure, sensor calibration details)
Figure labels omit key dimensions (tube height, sensor angle) affecting reproducibility
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission fully meets the 5â6 band for research question context by situating the study in a specific physical regime and real-world relevance. Methodological descriptions allow reproduction with only minor omissions, and methodological considerations are described rather than merely stated, aligning with the top band. The few ambiguities noted are insufficient to drop the criterion below 6.

strengths: Quantitative conclusion (Î» â A) is directly supported by regression results and high RÂ²
Conclusion is fully consistent with the processed data and clearly links back to the hypothesis
weaknesses: Comparison to accepted scientific context is limited and lacks quantitative literature values
Discussion of the non-zero intercept and theoretical implications is brief
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is well justified by the analysis and fully consistent with the data, meeting the topâband descriptor for relevance and support (5â6). The context comparison shows some relevant links but lacks depth and literature benchmarking, keeping the score at 5.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses and limitations are identified (elliptical swing, tube drag, string attachment)
Suggested improvements are realistic and directly relevant to key limitations
weaknesses: No quantitative assessment of which weakness most impacted Î» uncertainty
Improvements are described but not explained in terms of how they quantitatively reduce uncertainty or prioritised
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation describes specific limitations and proposes realistic improvements, aligning with the 3â4 band. However, the impact of these weaknesses is not quantitatively assessed, and improvements lack explanatory depth, placing this criterion at the lower end of the band (3).

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is clearly formulated and immediately followed by a focused introduction that situates the study within the physics of damping, distinguishes viscous from pressure drag, and justifies the simplification to low-speed viscous drag.  The student also links the work to real-world and personal motivation, demonstrating a **specific and appropriate context** rather than a broad, generic one.  Hence the descriptor for the top band is fulfilled.

Instrument uncertainties are quoted for radius, motion-sensor interval, and surface-area propagation; error bars appear on the final graph and are used for max/min gradient.  However, no uncertainty is attached to damping-coefficient values, nor is combined uncertainty propagated through the exponential fit.  These omissions mean consideration is present but incomplete, fitting band 3-4 with a lower score.

Controlled variables (string length, mass, launch amplitude, sensor geometry, room drafts) are identified and **described** with justification; the student explains why each affects damping.  Nonetheless, explanations are not consistently quantitative and some factors (tube drag, sensor alignment error) are recognised only later in evaluation.  This meets the descriptor for âdescribedâ rather than fully âexplained,â fitting band 3-4.

Specific weaknesses are **described** (elliptical swing, insecure string attachment, ignored tube drag, limited trials, radius cutting error).  The relative impact is mentioned qualitatively (could shift line from origin) but not quantitatively analysed, so the explanation depth is limited, satisfying band 3-4 at the lower end.

Step-by-step procedures, equipment lists with uncertainties, and diagrams make the set-up largely reproducible; however, some details remain ambiguous (e.g., exact string type/diameter, play-doh placement mass distribution, motion-sensor calibration).  These minor omissions place the work in the **âfew ambiguitiesâ** rather than fully unambiguous category, aligning with the 3-4 band.

Data processing (averaging peaks, exponential fitting to extract Î», linear regression of Î» vs A, calculation of propagated area uncertainties) is broadly correct and appropriate.  Minor inconsistencies (scientific notation stripped for plotting, sign conventions) slightly reduce accuracy but do not constitute major flaws.  Therefore processing meets the top-band descriptor, albeit not perfectly, warranting 5 rather than 6 marks.

Improvements are realistic and relevant (laser-cut discs, verify string length each trial, guide rails to suppress rocking, include mass uncertainties).  They are **described** but the student does not fully explain how each would improve accuracy/precision quantitatively or prioritise them.  This matches the descriptor for band 3-4.

Extensive raw-data tables, sample tables, averaged tables, and labelled graphs are provided.  The presentation is **clear** (logical order, headings, units) but not always precise (duplicated table titles, inconsistent significant figures, some truncated axis labels).  Thus communication is clear OR precise, not both, meriting band 3-4.

Some relevant comparison to accepted scientific context is made: theoretical derivation Î»âA from drag equations and comment on low-velocity viscous regime.  However, no quantitative literature values or deeper theoretical model (e.g., Reynolds number limits) are invoked, so justification remains partial.  This aligns with the middle band.

The student states a quantitative conclusion (Î»âA) that is directly extracted from the regression and discusses the high RÂ² value, clearly linking back to the hypothesis.  The conclusion is **fully consistent** with the processed data, though the discussion of the non-zero intercept is brief.  This largely satisfies the top-band descriptor; a small lack of depth keeps the score at 5 rather than 6.

annotations.comment: The introduction effectively situates the research question but would benefit from quantifying the low-velocity regime with a Reynolds number estimate to justify neglecting pressure drag.
The derivation equates drag equations assuming constant velocity v; this assumption needs justification or discussion of its impact on the exponential decay model.
Step 5 in âGathering dataâ instructs attachment of discs but does not specify mass-balancing procedure until later; consolidate these instructions for clarity.
Table 3 presents raw data with inconsistent significant figures for distances; standardize to 2 dp to match sensor resolution.
Graph 1 axes are truncated and lack grid ticks at key values, reducing precision in reading peak positions.
The schematic (Figure 1) lacks labels for key dimensions (tube height, sensor angle) and component dimensions, impeding reproducibility.
The description of string attributes omits diameter and material type, which affect drag and tension; include these details to ensure full reproducibility.
Equipment table lists âStringâ without specifying elasticity or diameter, which introduces uncontrolled variability; quantify these attributes and uncertainties.
The risk assessment clearly identifies major hazards and controls, showing thoughtful planning for safety and supervision.
No uncertainty is associated with the time readings from the motion sensor; include the Â±0.05 s resolution and consider systematic lag in peak detection.
Exponential fits in Graph 2 are shown without error bars or confidence intervals; include these to assess the reliability of Î» extraction.
The sign convention for Î» is inconsistent: negative drag coefficients should be defined physically, and absolute values reported if appropriate.
The discussion of RÂ² lacks interpretation of residuals or outlier influence; include residual plot or calculate ÏÂ² to strengthen correlation claims.
The averaging method for processed data is not described: clarify if arithmetic mean or median and include propagation of the standard deviation of the three trials.
Table 6 still uses mixed notation for Î» values; present all in consistent scientific format (e.g. Ã10â»Â²) for clarity.
Propagation of uncertainty for surface area relies on equation ÎA/A = 2Îr/r but rounding of ÎA to two significant figures needs justification to avoid underestimating uncertainty.
Table 7 includes surface area uncertainties but does not propagate these into gradient uncertainty in Graph 4; include error bars on both axes.
Graph 3 omits error bars on Î», undermining visual assessment of the origin intersection; include maximum/minimum regression lines with propagated uncertainties.
The conclusion restates the hypothesis confirmation but lacks comparison to literature values or theoretical predictions beyond proportionality.
The evaluation describes limitations but does not quantitatively assess which methodological weakness had the greatest impact on Î» uncertainty.
Scientific context comparison is limited: no quantitative literature drag coefficients are cited to benchmark experimental Î» values.
Suggested improvements (e.g., laser cutter, guide rails) are realistic but lack explanation of how each change quantitatively reduces uncertainty.
Including the mass balance uncertainty is an important suggestion; explain how mass uncertainty propagates into Î» via the inertia term.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
